questions.q: Maharana Pratap was the ruler of which kingdom?
questions.explanation: Maharana Pratap was the Sisodia Rajput ruler of Mewar.

questions.q: Maharana Pratap is best remembered for resisting which empire?
questions.explanation: Maharana Pratap resisted Mughal expansion under the emperor Akbar.

questions.q: Which Mughal emperor did Maharana Pratap oppose?
questions.explanation: Maharana Pratap stood against the expansion of Akbar's Mughal Empire.

questions.q: What was the name of Maharana Pratap's famous loyal horse?
questions.explanation: Chetak was the celebrated steed of Maharana Pratap, honoured for his loyalty in battle.

questions.q: Maharana Pratap is most associated with which famous battle?
questions.explanation: Maharana Pratap is celebrated for his stand at the Battle of Haldighati in 1576.

questions.q: To which Rajput clan did Maharana Pratap belong?
questions.explanation: Maharana Pratap belonged to the Sisodia clan, the rulers of Mewar.

questions.q: In which present-day Indian state did Maharana Pratap's kingdom of Mewar lie?
questions.explanation: Mewar lay in present-day Rajasthan in western India.

questions.q: In which century did Maharana Pratap rule Mewar?
questions.explanation: Maharana Pratap ruled Mewar in the late 16th century CE.

questions.q: Maharana Pratap became an enduring symbol of what?
questions.explanation: His refusal to submit to the Mughals made him an enduring symbol of resistance and defiance.

questions.q: What royal title was borne by the ruler of Mewar such as Pratap?
questions.explanation: The rulers of Mewar held the title Maharana, hence Maharana Pratap.

questions.q: Who was the father of Maharana Pratap, the Mewar ruler he succeeded?
questions.explanation: Maharana Pratap was the son of Udai Singh II, founder of the city of Udaipur.

questions.q: The city of Udaipur, associated with Mewar's later capital, was founded by whom?
questions.explanation: Udaipur was founded by Udai Singh II, Maharana Pratap's father, as a new capital for Mewar.

questions.q: In what year did Maharana Pratap fight the Battle of Haldighati against the Mughals?
questions.explanation: The Battle of Haldighati was fought in 1576 between Pratap and Akbar's forces.

questions.q: Which Mughal commander led Akbar's army against Maharana Pratap at Haldighati?
questions.explanation: Raja Man Singh of Amber led the Mughal army against Maharana Pratap at Haldighati.

questions.q: After Haldighati, Maharana Pratap continued resistance mainly through what kind of warfare?
questions.explanation: Pratap waged guerrilla warfare from the Aravalli hills to harass the Mughals after Haldighati.

questions.q: Which hill fort, long the Sisodia capital, had been lost to the Mughals before Pratap's reign, fuelling his resistance?
questions.explanation: Chittorgarh had fallen to Akbar in 1568, and recovering Mewar's honour drove Pratap's defiance.

questions.q: Which loyal chief of the Bhil people supported Maharana Pratap's struggle?
questions.explanation: The Bhil tribes of the Aravalli region were key allies of Maharana Pratap in his hill warfare.

questions.q: A loyal noble who is traditionally said to have impersonated Pratap to help him escape at Haldighati was?
questions.explanation: The Jhala chief is said to have taken Pratap's royal insignia to draw the enemy and let the Maharana escape.

questions.q: Maharana Pratap is said to have recovered much of Mewar later in his life through persistent campaigns. He died in which year?
questions.explanation: Maharana Pratap died in 1597 after recovering large parts of Mewar from Mughal control.

questions.q: Who succeeded Maharana Pratap as ruler of Mewar?
questions.explanation: Maharana Pratap was succeeded by his son Amar Singh I.

questions.q: A Muslim Afghan commander who fought on Maharana Pratap's side at Haldighati was?
questions.explanation: Hakim Khan Sur, an Afghan, led artillery for Maharana Pratap, showing the conflict was political rather than purely religious.

questions.q: Where did Maharana Pratap make his hill capital and base of operations during his resistance?
questions.explanation: Pratap established his later capital at Chavand and operated from Aravalli hill bases like Kumbhalgarh.

questions.q: In what year was Maharana Pratap born?
questions.explanation: Maharana Pratap was born in 1540, traditionally at Kumbhalgarh fort in Mewar.

questions.q: Maharana Pratap was crowned Maharana of Mewar in which year, succeeding amid a contested succession?
questions.explanation: Pratap was enthroned in 1572 after his father Udai Singh's death, despite a rival claim by his half-brother.

questions.q: Which half-brother of Maharana Pratap was initially favoured by their father and later joined Mughal service?
questions.explanation: Jagmal had been favoured by Udai Singh, but the nobles enthroned Pratap; Jagmal then defected to the Mughals.

questions.q: According to tradition, Maharana Pratap took a vow regarding his lifestyle until Mewar's honour was restored. What did this vow involve?
options: Living austerely, sleeping on straw and eating simply
questions.explanation: Legend holds that Pratap vowed to live austerely, sleeping on the ground and eating off leaf plates, until Mewar was free.

questions.q: Which wealthy minister is traditionally credited with funding Maharana Pratap's renewed campaigns in his later years?
questions.explanation: Bhamashah, Pratap's loyal minister, is said to have given his personal wealth to refinance the Mewar resistance.

questions.q: Near which present-day town in Rajasthan is the Battle of Haldighati pass located?
questions.explanation: The Haldighati pass lies in the Aravalli range near Rajsamand district, between Gogunda and Khamnor.

questions.q: Maharana Pratap's horse Chetak is most famously remembered for which feat during the retreat at Haldighati?
options: Carrying the wounded Maharana to safety across a stream before dying
Trampling Man Singh's elephant to death
Swimming a great river to Delhi
questions.explanation: Legend says the wounded Chetak leapt a stream to carry Pratap to safety and then collapsed and died.

questions.q: Which fort, Pratap's birthplace and a Sisodia stronghold, later became a key base in his hill resistance and is now a UNESCO site?
questions.explanation: Kumbhalgarh, traditionally Pratap's birthplace, was a major Mewar fortress and is a UNESCO World Heritage hill fort of Rajasthan.
